console.comment: The sqlite3 shell is a REPL: when launched through v86 execd it wants an interactive pty (cooked-mode line editing) so typed SQL reaches the shell and box/table results stream back. The runtime gate is execd's `interactive = true` (wasmmachine/execd-sqlite.toml). No termios/linenoise on wasip2 -> the shell's built-in local_getline line reader (cooked-mode echo + backspace via the pty line discipline; no in-app arrow-key history).

docs.summary: The real sqlite3 shell (v3.53.1) compiled to wasm as a wasi:cli/run component (scripts/build-shell-wasm.sh). Native CLI behaviour: box output, dot commands (.mode/.tables/.schema/.help/...), -box/-csv/-json/-line/-markdown, -readonly, -init. sqlite3 is linked statically so the component runs under plain `wasmtime`; runtime = "sqlink" runs it through `sqlink run-tool` with the host-import surface for a future `.load <name>` (PLAN-real-shell-tool.md P2).
